title: Pseuderanthemum tetrasepalum
taxonomy: Pseuderanthemum tetrasepalum belongs to the family Acanthaceae and is classified as a species of flowering plant. The genus Pseuderanthemum is part of the order Lamiales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. Pseuderanthemum tetrasepalum is a species of the Pseuderanthemum genus, which is native to tropical regions. Further classification can be determined by its specific traits and genetic information.

The plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal benefits.

parts: Pseuderanthemum tetrasepalum, also known as the False Fortune Plant, is a species of flowering plant used in traditional medicine. intro: Pseuderanthemum tetrasepalum, also known as the four-veined pseuderanthemum, is a plant species in the Acanthaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. The leaves of P. tetrasepalum have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its reported health benefits of reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, P. tetrasepalum is often made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds.
